name: obsidian-clipper-template-creator
description: Guide for creating templates for the Obsidian Web Clipper. Use when you want to create a new clipping template, understand available variables, or format clipped content.
capabilities: Provides a step-by-step workflow for creating Obsidian Web Clipper templates
Explains how to use template variables (preset, selector, schema, meta)
Shows how to analyze web pages and validate CSS selectors
Documents available filters for formatting extracted content
Includes example JSON templates for different use cases
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a documentation and guidance skill for creating Obsidian Web Clipper templates. All static scanner findings are false positives: backtick patterns in markdown are template variables, 'weak crypto' detections are double-brace template syntax, and hardcoded URLs are documentation links. No executable code, no network requests initiated by the skill, no file modifications.
valueStatement: This skill guides users through creating importable JSON templates for the Obsidian Web Clipper, enabling structured extraction of web content into your Obsidian vault.
limitations: Does not execute web clipping - only provides guidance for creating templates
Requires user to provide sample URLs for template verification
Does not modify or create files in the user's vault
Cannot access Obsidian directly - templates must be imported manually

useCases.targetUser: Obsidian users who want to organize video content
useCases.title: Create a YouTube Video Template
useCases.description: Build a template to clip YouTube videos with video title, channel name, duration, and thumbnail into your Obsidian vault.

useCases.targetUser: Home cooks who collect recipes in Obsidian
useCases.title: Create a Recipe Clipping Template
useCases.description: Design a template to extract recipe ingredients, instructions, and photos from cooking websites using Schema.org data.

useCases.title: Build a Research Article Template
useCases.description: Create a template to clip academic articles with title, authors, abstract, publication date, and source URL for research notes.

promptTemplates.title: Simple Template Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Obsidian Web Clipper template for clipping [TYPE OF CONTENT] from websites. I want to extract [WHAT DATA FIELDS]. Show me the JSON format.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner users who need basic template guidance

promptTemplates.title: Template with CSS Selectors
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to clip articles from [WEBSITE URL]. Analyze the page and create a template using CSS selectors to extract [SPECIFIC ELEMENTS]. Use WebFetch to analyze the page first.
promptTemplates.scenario: Users who need custom selectors for specific sites

promptTemplates.title: Schema.org Template
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a template that extracts structured data from [WEBSITE] using Schema.org JSON-LD. Focus on extracting [RECIPE/ARTICLE/PRODUCT] data fields. Verify the selectors work.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced users working with structured data

promptTemplates.title: Complex Multi-Section Template
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a comprehensive template for clipping [BLOG/WEBSITE] that extracts: 1) Main content with markdown formatting, 2) Author and date metadata, 3) Featured image, 4) Tags from meta tags. Include proper frontmatter and folder structure.

bestPractices: Always verify CSS selectors against actual page content using WebFetch before finalizing templates
Use stable selectors that are unlikely to change (data attributes, semantic HTML) rather than fragile class chains
Test templates with multiple pages from the same site to ensure selector reliability
antiPatterns: Guessing selectors without analyzing actual page HTML - this leads to broken templates
Using brittle class names that change with website updates
Skipping the verification step and creating templates that fail on actual use

faq.question: What is the Obsidian Web Clipper?
faq.answer: The Obsidian Web Clipper is a browser extension that lets you save web content directly into your Obsidian vault with customizable templates.

faq.question: Do I need programming knowledge to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance on creating templates. You just need to understand basic JSON structure and follow the workflow steps.

faq.question: Can this skill create templates for any website?
faq.answer: Yes, but the quality depends on the website structure. Some sites use Schema.org data making extraction easier, while others require custom CSS selectors.

faq.question: How do I import the created templates?
faq.answer: Copy the JSON output from this skill, then in Obsidian Web Clipper settings, choose 'Import template' and paste the JSON.

faq.question: What are preset variables?
faq.answer: Preset variables are automatically extracted data like {{title}}, {{url}}, {{content}}, {{author}}, and {{date}} that work on most web pages without configuration.

faq.question: How do I handle websites that block scraping?
faq.answer: This skill cannot bypass website restrictions. Some sites may block the Web Clipper or require authentication. You may need to use browser bookmarks or manual copying for those cases.
